# [Sub-Epic] Dynamic Topology — Unified vs. Federated Routing

## Objective
Enable seamless switching between a single-instance local ChromaDB deployment and a federated multi-instance cloud deployment via a unified configuration flag.

## Rationale
Solo developers use a single ChromaDB instance (port 8000). Enterprise teams may deploy KB and Memory Core as separate services behind an OAuth gateway. The topology flag (`NEO_CHROMA_UNIFIED`) must transparently route ChromaDB client connections without changing service logic.

## Scope
This sub-epic groups the following existing tickets:
- #10001 — Dynamic Topology: Unified vs. Federated Routing
- #10007 — Memory Core: Bypass Database Initialization in Unified Mode
- #10008 — Playwright Test Coverage: Unified Monolithic Topology
- #10009 — Playwright Test Coverage: Federated Cloud Topology

Parent Epic: #9999
Origin Session ID: 69b726ca-97c8-406c-9106-c63b6642c4c0

- 2026-04-23T23:32:12Z @tobiu referenced in commit `f70c48f` - "feat(memory-core): surface effective chromadb topology in /health (#10127) (#10268)

Extends HealthService.healthcheck() with a database.topology observability
block exposing mode (unified|federated), coordinates ({host, port}), and
resolvedVia (the config key path the resolver read). Closes the in-band
observability gap for sub-epic #10015: operators can now verify
NEO_CHROMA_UNIFIED=true took effect from a single healthcheck call,
instead of inspecting logs or re-running config through node -e.

- Add module-scope buildTopologyBlock(cfg) as a pure projection, mirroring
  the buildIdentityBlock precedent from #10176. Delegates coordinate
  resolution to ChromaManager.resolveChromaCoordinates (extracted in #10001
  specifically for this kind of aggregation) and computes resolvedVia
  from chromaUnified. Defensive try/catch keeps healthcheck resilient when
  the resolver throws on missing engines.kb.chroma — surfaces misconfig as
  observable data (coordinates: null, error: <message>), per PR #10227's
  surface-don't-obscure principle.
- Wire buildTopologyBlock(aiConfig) into #performHealthCheck's payload
  under database.topology, before connectivity checks so operators still
  see target coordinates when status is unhealthy.
- Extend HealthService.spec.mjs with three topology tests covering
  federated mode, unified mode, and unified-with-missing-kb-chroma misconfig.
- Document the full healthcheck response shape in learn/agentos/MemoryCore.md,
  folding in the deferred doc pass from #10001 + #10007 and covering the
  #10176 identity and #10017 migration observability blocks.

Resolves #10127
